channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “ radicals, zealots and Marxists.” He has picked Linda McMahon, a former wrestling executive, to lead the department.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you remove children from the harms of social media? Politically the answer appears simple in Australia, but practically the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 things to know about Linda McMahon, Trump's pick for education secretary
item.description: If confirmed by the Senate, McMahon will be put in charge of heading up changes to Title IX and determining how student debt is handled.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas board advances plan to allow Bible material in elementary school lessons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas' education board on Tuesday advanced a new Bible-infused curriculum that would be optional for schools to incorporate in kindergarten through fifth grades, one of the latest Republican-led efforts in the U.S. to incorporate more religious teaching into classrooms.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about Linda McMahon, Trump’s pick for Education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon has been a constant presence in Donald Trump's tumultuous orbit, serving in his first administration and supporting his presidential campaigns. Now he's chosen her to serve as Education secretary. Here's a look at McMahon's background, from business to politics.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump to nominate professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is nominating the billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead US, Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-Maryland teacher to serve fraction of 30-year sentence after pleading guilty to sex with teen student
item.description: A former Maryland middle school teacher was sentenced to prison on Friday after pleading guilty to having sex with a 14-year-old student dozens of times in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trial starts for New Hampshire youth center worker accused of holding down boy while he was raped
item.description: MANCHESTER, N.H. (AP) — A man who says he was brutally raped as a teenager by workers at a New Hampshire youth center testified Tuesday that he couldn't report the attack at the time because the perpetrators were responsible for his welfare. “They just did that to you.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Prosecutors finished calling witnesses Tuesday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, and the defense began presenting its case but quickly ran into snags. Jose Ibarra, 26, is charged with murder and other crimes in Riley’s February death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Veteran, 98, finally receives medals earned in World War II
item.description: Rhode Island Senator Jack Reed hosted a special medal ceremony at his office in Cranston to present John DeLeo with his "overdue" military honors.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: WWII soldier's remains back home after 80 years
item.description: "Thank goodness for the kindness of the German people for burying him intact and with dignity," Zarifian's obituary reads.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Brill, NewsNation

item.title: 51-year-old man is charged with murder after 3 are stabbed in New York City rampage
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man has been charged with three counts of first-degree murder in the fatal stabbings of three people in a string of random attacks across Manhattan, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rare coin found in piece of furniture sells for $2.52M at auction
item.description: A rare 17th Century coin sold at auction for a record-breaking $2.52 million eight years after it was found in an old cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Tracking the Tropics | 2024 hurricane season nears its end
item.description: With just two weeks left to go in the 2024 hurricane season, the tropics appear to have quieted down.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Carcass of endangered fin whale washes up near Alaska's largest city
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An endangered fin whale that washed up near a coastal trail in Alaska's largest city has attracted curious onlookers while biologists seek answers as to what caused the animal's death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump picks Dr. Oz to run Medicare and Medicaid, Linda McMahon for Education, Lutnick for Commerce
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has tapped billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apartment buildings wrap up $1B in HUD funds for lower utility bills and climate upgrades
item.description: Renters in affordable housing felt forgotten and left out of the nation's transition to clean energy. They lived in older buildings that had been repaired over the years but had leaky windows and old appliances that consumed a lot of energy. They didn't have solar power.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marines who served with Daniel Penny take witness stand in chokehold trial, reveal Humanitarian Service Medal
item.description: Daniel Penny's mother, former Marine sergeants and an airline pilot friend took the witness stand Tuesday in his subway chokehold trial in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, Fox 5

item.title: Mississippi restaurant, co-owner sentenced for decades-long fish mislabeling scheme
item.description: GULFPORT, Miss. (WJTV) - A Mississippi corporation operating as Mary Mahoney’s Old French House restaurant in Biloxi, Mississippi, and its co-owner and manager were sentenced on charges for a long-standing conspiracy to misbrand seafood.
item.dc:creator: Sethanie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Mountain lion rescued from tree outside California home
item.description: First responders rescued a young mountain lion from a tree after he wandered into the yard of an Orange County home on Monday afternoon
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Squatters meet resistance with red state push to protect homeowners
item.description: A prospective Wyoming law would make squatting that involves property damage a felony offense punishable by up to 10 years and a $10,000 fine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bragg's office vies to suppress Jordan Neely's drug abuse, psyche records in Marine vet's chokehold trial
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors do not want Daniel Penny's jurors to hear from a defense expert who would weigh in on Jordan Neely's drug use and psychiatric outbursts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 killed in series of random stabbings in New York City
item.description: A man is accused of fatally stabbing three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Gov Newsom announces decision in Menendez brothers case
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he will respect the will of the voters after they replaced the Los Angeles district attorney who made a last-minute push in case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied longer range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike deeper inside Russia, easing limitations on the longer range weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Veteran news executive Reg Murphy, who survived abduction decades ago, has died at 90
item.description: Reg Murphy, a renowned journalist whose newsgathering career included stints as an editor and top executive at newspapers in Atlanta, San Francisco and Baltimore — and who found himself the subject of national headlines when he survived a politically motivated kidnapping — has died at age 90.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: Just days after being tapped to co-lead the Department of Government Efficiency, Elon Musk is searching for "high-IQ" individuals to join the taskforce.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9
